labels.notes: Linda Guest 9:53am-11:00am (DOB 1951-04-18) — TWO distinct patients incorrectly merged into one session bdb55313. Linda received epidural at L5-S1 + SI joint + GTB bursa injections. Then Rashida Rehman Hafiz-zadeh (vision tracker confirmed at 6 unanimous votes) checked in for IIH suspect, prescribed acetazolamide. The encounter_merge LLM at 15:00:48 collapsed Rashida's session a0d2cf0b BACK INTO Linda's bdb55313, citing 'continuous conversation immediately following the written documentation' — but it was looking only at the first 500 words of a0d2cf0b which were Linda's post-procedure debrief. Rashida's IIH+acetazolamide content arrived later in the same buffer and survived the merge into the SOAP regen output, producing a Frankenstein SOAP. ROOT CAUSES (Class A merge engine identity-blind + window-biased): (1) merge prompt builds with `patient_name: null` even though prev_encounter_patient_name='Linda Guest' AND name_tracker.majority_name='Rashida Rehman Hafiz-zadeh' (6 unanimous votes) were both available at merge time; (2) merge LLM only sees curr_head_words=500 — cannot detect mid-buffer patient transitions; (3) post-merge multi_patient_detect at 15:02:27 (the safety net) FAILED with 30s timeout on the long combined transcript. Diagnostic code 401 (Essential hypertension) is wildly wrong — should be 729/724 for the back/SI/GTB pain (Class B dx semantic guard rejected good 724 from tools-model). Billing missed the epidural (G231A, no `epidural` keyword in augment table) and undercounted the additional joints (3 injections billed as G370A+G371A=2 sites; should be G370A+2×G371A or G231A+G370A+G371A — Class E billing under-extraction).
